name: The Flynn
hint: The 1930 art-deco marquee that anchors lower Main Street.
author: Beyond My Ken

name: Burlington City Hall
hint: Brick-and-marble 1928 McKim, Mead & White civic pile at the bottom of the Marketplace.
author: Beyond My Ken

name: BCA Center (old Firehouse)
hint: Ethan Allen Engine Co. No. 4 rolled out of here; art hangs here now.
author: Beyond My Ken

name: Old Post Office & Custom House
hint: Granite federal building at Main & Church, from 1906.
author: Beyond My Ken

name: Fletcher Free Library
hint: Carnegie-funded and nearly lost to a sinking foundation in the 1970s.
author: Beyond My Ken

name: Memorial Auditorium
hint: Built 1928 to honor WWI dead; shuttered since 2016 and still argued about.
author: Beyond My Ken

name: 123 Church Street
hint: A 1928 bank temple turned Marketplace storefront.
author: Beyond My Ken

name: Church Street at Bank
hint: Middle block of the Marketplace, looking toward City Hall.
author: Beyond My Ken

name: Upper Church Street
hint: Top block of the Marketplace in peak foliage.
author: Sdkb

name: Henry's Diner
hint: Slinging eggs on Bank Street since 1925.
author: Farragutful

name: Ben & Jerry's
hint: The scoop shop near where it all started (the real first one was a gas station down the hill).
author: G. Edward Johnson

name: Leunig's Bistro
hint: Parisian-ish corner café at Church & College.
author: G. Edward Johnson

name: Rí Rá Irish Pub
hint: Guinness in a former bank building on the Marketplace.
author: G. Edward Johnson

name: Homeport
hint: The kitchen-and-everything-else store, a Church Street fixture since 1977.
author: G. Edward Johnson

name: CVS on Church Street
hint: Even the chain drugstore gets a historic brick facade here.
author: Harrison Keely

name: First Unitarian Universalist
hint: The 1816 meeting house that closes the view up Church Street.
author: Beyond My Ken

name: Masonic Temple
hint: Fortress-y 1898 landmark at Church & Pearl.
author: Farragutful

name: Richardson Building
hint: Ornate 1895 department-store block at Church & Cherry.
author: Beyond My Ken

name: Radio Bean
hint: Coffee, tiny stages, and late nights on North Winooski.
author: Artaxerxes

name: The Other Place
hint: The neighborhood dive next door to the Bean.
author: Artaxerxes

name: First Baptist Church
hint: 1864 brick steeple on St. Paul Street.
author: Farragutful

name: Former Immaculate Conception Cathedral
hint: The modernist 1977 cathedral and its famous grove of locust trees.
author: Farragutful

name: Cathedral Church of St. Paul
hint: Brutalist Episcopal cathedral rebuilt after the 1971 fire.
author: Farragutful

name: Hotel Vermont
hint: Cherry Street boutique hotel, very Vermont about it.
author: Farragutful

name: Costello Courthouse
hint: Where Chittenden County goes to court, on Cherry Street.
author: Farragutful

name: Downtown Transit Center
hint: Every GMT route in the county funnels through here.
author: Farragutful

name: Zampieri State Office Building
hint: The state government’s downtown outpost on Pearl Street.
author: Farragutful

name: 100 Bank Street
hint: One of the few things downtown you could call a high-rise.
author: Farragutful

name: Wells-Richardson Building
hint: A patent-medicine fortune built this College Street block.
author: G. Edward Johnson

name: The Nest (CityPlace)
hint: The tower that finally rose out of the famous pit.
author: MrFib

name: Decker Towers
hint: Vermont’s tallest building, an 11-story housing high-rise.
author: MrFib

name: The Armory
hint: Castle-ish 1905 drill hall on lower Main Street.
author: Beyond My Ken

name: Edmunds School
hint: The grand 1900 school building up Main Street hill.
author: Beyond My Ken

name: College Street Congregational
hint: Red-brick 1866 church with the green steeple.
author: Farragutful

name: First Congregational Church
hint: The 1842 columned meetinghouse on South Winooski.
author: Farragutful

name: First United Methodist
hint: Stone church at Buell and South Winooski.
author: Farragutful

name: Poco
hint: Small plates at the bottom of Main Street.
author: Artaxerxes

name: Follett House
hint: Greek Revival mansion staring down the lake from College Street.
author: Beyond My Ken

name: Union Station
hint: The 1916 beaux-arts depot at the foot of Main — wings on the roof.
author: Beyond My Ken

name: Main Street Landing
hint: Colorful waterfront arts building on Lake Street.
author: Beyond My Ken

name: Skinny Pancake
hint: Crêpes by the waterfront.
author: G. Edward Johnson

name: Spirit of Ethan Allen III
hint: The lake cruise boat at its Burlington Boathouse dock.
author: Tony Hisgett from Birmingham, UK

name: Battery Park
hint: Cannons fired at the British from this bluff in 1813.
author: Niranjan Arminius

name: Chief Grey Lock statue
hint: The Abenaki war leader watches the lake from Battery Park.
author: Sculptor: Peter Wolf Toth / Photo: Niranjan Arminius

name: Waterfront Park
hint: Rail yard turned festival lawn on Lake Champlain.
author: Artaxerxes

name: St. Joseph Co-Cathedral
hint: The French-Canadian cathedral towering over Allen Street.
author: Farragutful

name: Elmwood Cemetery
hint: Burlington’s oldest burying ground, 1810.
author: Farragutful

name: North Street
hint: The Old North End’s main drag.
author: Mfwills

name: Ethan Allen Homestead
hint: Where the Green Mountain Boy actually lived, out in the Intervale.

name: St. Mark’s
hint: New North End parish way up North Avenue.
author: Farragutful

name: UVM Old Mill
hint: UVM’s oldest building; Lafayette laid the cornerstone.

name: UVM Morrill Hall
hint: Named for the Vermont senator behind land-grant colleges.

name: UVM Williams Hall
hint: The gargoyle-adjacent Romanesque science hall on the Green.

name: UVM Billings Library
hint: H.H. Richardson designed this one — the fancy library.
author: HeyTomek

name: Ira Allen Chapel
hint: The campus chapel with the landmark brick tower.
author: HeyTomek

name: UVM Davis Center
hint: The student center bridging Main Street and campus.
author: HeyTomek

name: Royall Tyler Theatre
hint: UVM’s playhouse in a converted 1901 gym.

name: Lafayette Statue
hint: The Marquis himself, at the top of the UVM Green.

name: UVM Converse Hall
hint: The Hogwarts-looking dorm on East Campus.
author: Niranjan Arminius

name: Gutterson Fieldhouse
hint: Home ice of Catamount hockey since 1963.

name: Grasse Mount
hint: Federal-style 1804 mansion partway up Main Street hill.

name: World’s Tallest Filing Cabinet
hint: Bren Alvarez’s 38-drawer monument to bureaucratic delay.

hint: Parish church at Flynn and Pine in the South End.

hint: Church and school off Shelburne Road.
author: Farragutful

hint: The universally accessible treehouse in Oakledge Park.

name: Lakeside neighborhood
hint: Company housing built for Queen City Cotton workers.

name: Burlington International Airport
hint: BTV — technically in South Burlington, spiritually everywhere.

hint: The new downtown South Burlington built for itself on Market Street.

hint: The Onion City announces itself.
